claims.claim: Kazakhstan has signed an agreement providing 165,000 educators with access to ChatGPT Edu
claims.evidence: Freedom Holding Corp press release confirms '165,000 educators in Kazakhstan will gain access to ChatGPT Edu'

claims.claim: ChatGPT Edu is designed with strengthened privacy and data protection controls
claims.evidence: Freedom Holding Corp and OpenAI official materials confirm 'ChatGPT Edu — an enhanced educational edition of ChatGPT designed with strengthened privacy and data protection controls'

claims.claim: The tool is expected to streamline lesson planning, improve assessment processes, and support individualized learning
claims.evidence: Times of Central Asia reports: 'The introduction of ChatGPT Edu is expected to streamline lesson planning, improve assessment processes, and support individualized learning'

claims.claim: Kazakhstan will become one of the first countries to use ChatGPT Edu at a national scale across primary, secondary, tertiary education
claims.evidence: EdTech Innovation Hub confirms: 'Kazakhstan will now become one of the first countries in the world to use ChatGPT Edu at a national scale'

concerns.detail: Listed source is NYT article on Estonia/Iceland (2026-01-02), but reporting brief cites Freedom Holding Corp, Times of Central Asia, EdTech Innovation Hub. Confirm whether original reporting is from these sources, not NYT.
Articles confirm agreement signed, but actual rollout date, teacher training program details, and cost structure remain unverified. When does distribution begin? What's the timeline?

concerns.issue: ChatGPT Edu privacy features not independently audited
concerns.detail: OpenAI claims about privacy are taken at face value; independent security/privacy audit results not found in reporting

draft.headline: 카자흐스탄, 16.5만 교사에 ChatGPT Edu 제공 협약
draft.subheadline: OpenAI의 중앙아시아 교육 진출, 개인정보보호 강화 버전으로 시작

audit_log.note: NYT 원본 접근 불가하나 Freedom Holding Corp, Times of Central Asia 등 3개 신뢰 소스 확보
전체 신뢰도 82, FLAG — 원본 NYT 소스 확인 필요, 도입 일정 및 세부 구현 계획 미확인
